info.description:
Datadog v2 REST API for logs, metrics, incidents, security, CI visibility, and observability pipelines in the Datadog platform.

Authentication: API key in DD-API-KEY header and application key in DD-APPLICATION-KEY header — generate both from Datadog Organization Settings → API Keys and Application Keys.

Source: https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/v2/
info.title: Datadog
info.x-itential-curated: Trimmed to 39 of 1198 upstream operations covering common CRUD for observability automation: downtimes, maintenance windows, incidents (create/get/search), log event submission and search, metrics listing and querying (scalar/timeseries), tag enrichment rules, users, and roles. See the repo README for the full scope and the full spec.

site.description: The regional site for Datadog customers.

subdomain.description: The subdomain where the API is deployed.

name.description: Full site DNS name.

protocol.description: The protocol for accessing the API.

subdomain.description: The subdomain where the API is deployed.

parameters.description: Only return downtimes that are active when the request is made.
parameters.name: current_only

parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of resource paths for related resources to include in the response. Supported resource
paths are `created_by` and `monitor`.
parameters.name: include

parameters.description: Maximum number of downtimes in the response.
parameters.name: page[limit]

delete.description:
Cancel a downtime.

**Note**: Downtimes canceled through the API are no longer active, but are retained for approximately two days before being permanently removed. The downtime may still appear in search results until it is permanently removed.

parameters.description: ID of the downtime to cancel.
parameters.name: downtime_id

parameters.description: ID of the downtime to fetch.
parameters.name: downtime_id

parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of resource paths for related resources to include in the response. Supported resource
paths are `created_by` and `monitor`.
parameters.name: include

parameters.description: ID of the downtime to update.
parameters.name: downtime_id

get.description:
List endpoint returns logs that match a log search query.
[Results are paginated][1].

Use this endpoint to search and filter your logs.

**If you are considering archiving logs for your organization,
consider use of the Datadog archive capabilities instead of the log list API.
See [Datadog Logs Archive documentation][2].**

[1]: /logs/guide/collect-multiple-logs-with-pagination
[2]: https://docs.datadoghq.com/logs/archives

parameters.description: Search query following logs syntax.
parameters.name: filter[query]

parameters.description:
For customers with multiple indexes, the indexes to search.
Defaults to '*' which means all indexes
parameters.name: filter[indexes]

items.description: The name of a log index.

parameters.description: Minimum timestamp for requested logs.
parameters.name: filter[from]

parameters.description: Maximum timestamp for requested logs.
parameters.name: filter[to]

parameters.description: Specifies the storage type to be used
parameters.name: filter[storage_tier]

parameters.description: Order of logs in results.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: List following results with a cursor provided in the previous query.
parameters.name: page[cursor]

parameters.description: Maximum number of logs in the response.
parameters.name: page[limit]

post.description:
List endpoint returns logs that match a log search query.
[Results are paginated][1].

Use this endpoint to search and filter your logs.

**If you are considering archiving logs for your organization,
consider use of the Datadog archive capabilities instead of the log list API.
See [Datadog Logs Archive documentation][2].**

[1]: /logs/guide/collect-multiple-logs-with-pagination
[2]: https://docs.datadoghq.com/logs/archives

get.description: Returns all configured maintenance windows for event management cases. Maintenance windows define time periods during which case notifications and automation rules are suppressed for cases matching a given query.

**Note**: This endpoint is in preview and is subject to change.
If you have any feedback, contact [Datadog support](https://docs.datadoghq.com/help/).

get.description:
Get a list of actively reporting metrics for your organization. Pagination is optional using the `page[cursor]` and `page[size]` query parameters.

Query parameters use bracket notation (for example, `filter[tags]`, `filter[queried][window][seconds]`). Pass them as standard URL query strings, URL-encoding the brackets if your client does not handle them. For example: `GET /api/v2/metrics?filter[tags]=env:prod&window[seconds]=86400&page[size]=500`.

parameters.description: Only return custom metrics that have been configured with Metrics Without Limits.
parameters.name: filter[configured]

parameters.description: Only return metrics that have the given tag key(s) in their Metrics Without Limits configuration (included or excluded).
parameters.name: filter[tags_configured]

parameters.description: Only return metrics of the given metric type.
parameters.name: filter[metric_type]

parameters.description: Only return distribution metrics that have percentile aggregations enabled (true) or disabled (false).
parameters.name: filter[include_percentiles]

parameters.description: Only return metrics that have been queried (true) or not queried (false) in the look back window. Set the window with `filter[queried][window][seconds]`; if omitted, a default window is used.
parameters.name: filter[queried]

parameters.description: This parameter has no effect unless `filter[queried]` is also set. Only return metrics that have been queried or not queried in the specified window. The default value is 2,592,000 seconds (30 days), the maximum value is 15,552,000 seconds (180 days), and the minimum value is 1 second. For example: `filter[queried]=true&filter[queried][window][seconds]=604800`.
parameters.name: filter[queried][window][seconds]

parameters.description: Only return metrics that were submitted with tags matching this expression. You can use AND, OR, IN, and wildcards. For example: `filter[tags]=env IN (staging,test) AND service:web*`.
parameters.name: filter[tags]

parameters.description: Only return metrics that are used in at least one dashboard, monitor, notebook, or SLO.
parameters.name: filter[related_assets]

parameters.description: Only return metrics that have been actively reporting in the specified window. The default value is 3600 seconds (1 hour), the maximum value is 2,592,000 seconds (30 days), and the minimum value is 1 second.
parameters.name: window[seconds]

parameters.description: Maximum number of results per page. Send `page[size]` on the first request to opt in to pagination. On each subsequent request, send `page[cursor]` set to the value of `meta.pagination.next_cursor` from the previous response. The default value is 10000, the maximum value is 10000, and the minimum value is 1.
parameters.name: page[size]

parameters.description: Cursor for pagination. Use `page[size]` to opt-in to pagination and get the first page; for subsequent pages, use the value from `meta.pagination.next_cursor` in the response. Pagination is complete when `next_cursor` is null.
parameters.name: page[cursor]

post.description:
Query scalar values (as seen on Query Value, Table, and Toplist widgets).
Multiple data sources are supported with the ability to
process the data using formulas and functions.
post.summary: Query scalar data across multiple products

post.description:
Query timeseries data across various data sources and
process the data by applying formulas and functions.
post.summary: Query timeseries data across multiple products

get.description: Returns all roles, including their names and their unique identifiers.

parameters.description:
Sort roles depending on the given field. Sort order is **ascending** by default.
Sort order is **descending** if the field is prefixed by a negative sign, for example:
`sort=-name`.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Filter all roles by the given string.
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Filter all roles by the given list of role IDs.
parameters.name: filter[id]

post.description: Create a new role for your organization.

get.description: Get a role in the organization specified by the role’s `role_id`.

patch.description: Edit a role. Can only be used with application keys belonging to administrators.

get.description: List all tag pipeline rulesets - Retrieve a list of all tag pipeline rulesets for the organization

post.description: Create a new tag pipeline ruleset with the specified rules and configuration
Reorder tag pipeline rulesets - Change the execution order of tag pipeline rulesets

get.description: List the processing status of all tag pipeline rulesets. Returns only the ID and processing status for each ruleset.

post.description: Validate a tag pipeline query - Validate the syntax and structure of a tag pipeline query

delete.description: Delete a tag pipeline ruleset - Delete an existing tag pipeline ruleset by its ID

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the ruleset
parameters.name: ruleset_id

get.description: Get a specific tag pipeline ruleset - Retrieve a specific tag pipeline ruleset by its ID

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the ruleset
parameters.name: ruleset_id

patch.description: Update a tag pipeline ruleset - Update an existing tag pipeline ruleset with new rules and configuration

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the ruleset
parameters.name: ruleset_id

rules.name: New table rule with new UI

get.description:
Get the list of all users in the organization. This list includes
all users even if they are deactivated or unverified.

parameters.description:
User attribute to order results by. Sort order is ascending by default.
Sort order is descending if the field
is prefixed by a negative sign, for example `sort=-name`. Options: `name`,
`modified_at`, `user_count`.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Direction of sort. Options: `asc`, `desc`.
parameters.name: sort_dir

parameters.description: Filter all users by the given string. Defaults to no filtering.
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description:
Filter on status attribute.
Comma separated list, with possible values `Active`, `Pending`, and `Disabled`.
Defaults to no filtering.
parameters.name: filter[status]

post.description: Create a user for your organization.

delete.description:
Disable a user. Can only be used with an application key belonging
to an administrator user.

get.description: Get a user in the organization specified by the user’s `user_id`.

patch.description:
Edit a user. Can only be used with an application key belonging
to an administrator user.

delete.description:
Cancel all pending invitations for a specified user.
Requires the `user_access_invite` permission.

parameters.description: The UUID of the user whose pending invitations should be canceled.
parameters.name: user_id

IncidentSearchSortQueryParameter.description: Specifies the order of returned incidents.
IncidentSearchSortQueryParameter.name: sort

IncidentSearchQueryQueryParameter.description:
Specifies which incidents should be returned. The query can contain any number of incident facets
joined by `ANDs`, along with multiple values for each of those facets joined by `OR`s. For
example: `state:active AND severity:(SEV-2 OR SEV-1)`.
IncidentSearchQueryQueryParameter.name: query

IncidentSearchIncludeQueryParameter.name: include

PageOffset.description: Specific offset to use as the beginning of the returned page.
PageOffset.name: page[offset]

MaintenanceWindowIDPathParameter.name: maintenance_window_id

When set to false, the configuration will include the configured tags and exclude any other submitted tags.
Defaults to false. Requires `tags` property.

Toggle to include or exclude percentile aggregations for distribution metrics.

LogsQueryOptions.description:
Global query options that are used during the query.
Note: These fields are currently deprecated and do not affect the query results.

timeOffset.description: The time offset (in seconds) to apply to the query.

timezone.description: The timezone can be specified as GMT, UTC, an offset from UTC (like UTC+1), or as a Timezone Database identifier (like America/New_York).
The timezone in which to schedule the downtime.

EventsTimeseriesQuery.description: An individual timeseries query for logs, RUM, traces, CI pipelines, security signals, and other event-based data sources. Use this query type for any data source powered by the Events Platform. See the data_source field for the full list of supported sources.

DowntimeScheduleCurrentDowntimeResponse.description:
The most recent actual start and end dates for a recurring downtime. For a canceled downtime,
this is the previously occurring downtime. For active downtimes, this is the ongoing downtime, and for scheduled
downtimes it is the upcoming downtime.

end.description: The end of the current downtime.

start.description: The start of the current downtime.

IncidentResponseIncludedItem.description: An object related to an incident that is included in the response.

DowntimeNotifyEndStateTypes.description: State that will trigger a monitor notification when the `notify_end_types` action occurs.

group_by.description: Tag keys to group results by.

items.description: A tag key to group by.

name.description: The variable name for use in formulas.

primary_tag_name.description: Name of the second primary tag used within APM. Required when `primary_tag_value` is specified. See https://docs.datadoghq.com/tracing/guide/setting_primary_tags_to_scope/#add-a-second-primary-tag-in-datadog

primary_tag_value.description: Value of the second primary tag by which to filter APM data. `primary_tag_name` must also be specified.

resource_name.description: The resource name to filter by.

service.description: The service name to filter by.

LogAttributes.description: JSON object containing all log attributes and their associated values.

attributes.description: JSON object of attributes from your log.

host.description: Name of the machine from where the logs are being sent.

message.description:
The message [reserved attribute](https://docs.datadoghq.com/logs/log_collection/#reserved-attributes)
of your log. By default, Datadog ingests the value of the message attribute as the body of the log entry.
That value is then highlighted and displayed in the Logstream, where it is indexed for full text search.

service.description:
The name of the application or service generating the log events.
It is used to switch from Logs to APM, so make sure you define the same
value when you use both products.

status.description: Status of the message associated with your log.

tags.description: Array of tags associated with your log.

items.description: Tag associated with your log.

GroupTags.description: List of tags that apply to a single response value.

items.description: A single tag that applies to a single response value.

ScalarColumn.description: A single column in a scalar query response.

ApmMetricsSpanKind.description: Describes the relationship between the span, its parents, and its children in a trace.

ApmResourceStatsDataSource.description: A data source for APM resource statistics queries.

next_offset.description: The index of the first element in the next page of results. Equal to page size added to the current offset.

offset.description: The index of the first element in the results.

MaintenanceWindowAttributes.description: Attributes of a maintenance window, including its schedule and the query that determines which cases are affected.

query.description: A case search query that determines which cases are affected during the maintenance window. Uses the same syntax as the Case Management search bar.

start_at.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp when the maintenance window begins and notifications start being suppressed.

updated_by.description: The UUID of the user who last modified this maintenance window. Read-only.

handle.description: The handle used for the notification. This includes an email address, Slack channel, or workflow.

SloDataSource.description: A data source for SLO queries.

family.description: Unit family, allows for conversion between units of the same family, for scaling.

name.description: Unit name

plural.description: Plural form of the unit name.

scale_factor.description: Factor for scaling between units of the same family.

IncidentFieldAttributesSingleValue.description: A field with a single value selected.

value.description: The single value selected for this field.

A unique identifier that represents the impact.

RoleUpdateData.description: Data related to the update of a role.

items.description: A source key for the reference table lookup.

QueryFormula.description: A formula for calculation based on one or more queries.

formula.description: Formula string, referencing one or more queries with their name property.

EventsScalarQuery.description: An individual scalar query for logs, RUM, traces, CI pipelines, security signals, and other event-based data sources. Use this query type for any data source powered by the Events Platform. See the data_source field for the full list of supported sources.

indexes.description: The indexes in which to search.

items.description: The unique index name.

name.description: The variable name for use in formulas.

TimeseriesResponseSeries.description: A single series in a timeseries query response, containing the query index, unit information, and group tags.

query_index.description: The index of the query in the "formulas" array (or "queries" array if no "formulas" was specified).

unit.description:
Detailed information about the unit.
The first element describes the "primary unit" (for example, `bytes` in `bytes per second`).
The second element describes the "per unit" (for example, `second` in `bytes per second`).
If the second element is not present, the API returns null.

name.description: The name of the user.

MaintenanceWindow.description: A maintenance window that defines a scheduled time period during which case-related notifications and automation rules are suppressed. Each maintenance window applies to cases matching a specified query.

ProcessDataSource.description: A data source for process-level infrastructure metrics.

MetricCustomSpaceAggregation.description: A space aggregation for use in query.

created_at.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp of when the user account was created.

disabled.description: Whether the user account is deactivated. Disabled users cannot log in.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for login and notifications.

handle.description: The unique handle (username) of the user, typically matching their email prefix.

icon.description: URL of the user's profile icon, typically a Gravatar URL derived from the email address.

last_login_time.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp of the user's most recent login, or null if the user has never logged in.

mfa_enabled.description: Whether multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enabled for the user's account.

modified_at.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp of when the user account was last modified.

service_account.description:
Whether this is a service account rather than a human user.
Service accounts are used for programmatic API access.

status.description: The current status of the user account (for example, `Active`, `Pending`, or `Disabled`).

title.description: The job title of the user (for example, "Senior Engineer" or "Product Manager").

uuid.description: The globally unique identifier (UUID) of the user.

verified.description: Whether the user's email address has been verified.

DowntimeScheduleResponse.description:
The schedule that defines when the monitor starts, stops, and recurs. There are two types of schedules:
one-time and recurring. Recurring schedules may have up to five RRULE-based recurrences. If no schedules are
provided, the downtime will begin immediately and never end.

A time interval in milliseconds.
May be overridden by a larger interval if the query would result in
too many points for the specified timeframe.
Defaults to a reasonable interval for the given timeframe.

to.description: End date (exclusive) of the query in milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

DowntimeScheduleRecurrenceRrule.description:
The `RRULE` standard for defining recurring events.
For example, to have a recurring event on the first day of each month, set the type to `rrule` and set the `FREQ` to `MONTHLY` and `BYMONTHDAY` to `1`.
Most common `rrule` options from the [iCalendar Spec](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5545) are supported.

**Note**: Attributes specifying the duration in `RRULE` are not supported (for example, `DTSTART`, `DTEND`, `DURATION`).
More examples available in this [downtime guide](https://docs.datadoghq.com/monitors/guide/suppress-alert-with-downtimes/?tab=api).

end.description:
ISO-8601 Datetime to end the downtime. Must include a UTC offset of zero. If not provided, the
downtime continues forever.

start.description:
ISO-8601 Datetime to start the downtime. Must include a UTC offset of zero. If not provided, the
downtime starts the moment it is created.

IncidentResponseAttributes.description: The incident's attributes from a response.

archived.description: Timestamp of when the incident was archived.

created.description: Timestamp when the incident was created.

customer_impact_duration.description:
Length of the incident's customer impact in seconds.
Equals the difference between `customer_impact_start` and `customer_impact_end`.

customer_impact_end.description: Timestamp when customers were no longer impacted by the incident.

customer_impact_scope.description: A summary of the impact customers experienced during the incident.

customer_impact_start.description: Timestamp when customers began being impacted by the incident.

customer_impacted.description: A flag indicating whether the incident caused customer impact.

declared.description: Timestamp when the incident was declared.

declared_by_uuid.description: UUID of the user who declared the incident.

detected.description: Timestamp when the incident was detected.

fields.description: A condensed view of the user-defined fields attached to incidents.

incident_type_uuid.description: A unique identifier that represents an incident type.

is_test.description: A flag indicating whether the incident is a test incident.

modified.description: Timestamp when the incident was last modified.

notification_handles.description: Notification handles that will be notified of the incident during update.

public_id.description: The monotonically increasing integer ID for the incident.

resolved.description: Timestamp when the incident's state was last changed from active or stable to resolved or completed.

time_to_detect.description:
The amount of time in seconds to detect the incident.
Equals the difference between `customer_impact_start` and `detected`.

time_to_internal_response.description: The amount of time in seconds to call incident after detection. Equals the difference of `detected` and `created`.

time_to_repair.description: The amount of time in seconds to resolve customer impact after detecting the issue. Equals the difference between `customer_impact_end` and `detected`.

time_to_resolve.description: The amount of time in seconds to resolve the incident after it was created. Equals the difference between `created` and `resolved`.

title.description: The title of the incident, which summarizes what happened.

If the second element is not present, the API returns null.

A list of monitor tags. For example, tags that are applied directly to monitors,
not tags that are used in monitor queries (which are filtered by the scope parameter), to which the downtime applies.
The resulting downtime applies to monitors that match **all** provided monitor tags. Setting `monitor_tags`
to `[*]` configures the downtime to mute all monitors for the given scope.

DowntimeResponse.description:
Downtiming gives you greater control over monitor notifications by
allowing you to globally exclude scopes from alerting.
Downtime settings, which can be scheduled with start and end times,
prevent all alerting related to specified Datadog tags.

name.description: The variable name for use in formulas.

primary_tag_name.description: The name of the second primary tag used within APM; required when `primary_tag_value` is specified. See https://docs.datadoghq.com/tracing/guide/setting_primary_tags_to_scope/#add-a-second-primary-tag-in-datadog.

primary_tag_value.description: Filter APM data by the second primary tag. `primary_tag_name` must also be specified.

resource_name.description: The resource name to filter by.

service.description: The service name to filter by.

disabled.description:
When set to `true`, the user is deactivated and can no longer log in.
When `false`, the user is active.

email.description:
The email address of the user, used for login and notifications.
Must be a valid email format.

name.description:
The full display name of the user as shown in the Datadog UI.
Maximum 55 characters, cannot contain `<` or `>`.

title.description: The job title of the user (for example, "Senior Engineer" or "Product Manager").
